# Jiawei's Blog

> Notes on LLMs, deep learning, and building things that work. Paper deep-dives, engineering notes, and data-science practice.

## Index

- [Home](https://cuijiawei.cn/) - Latest posts and writing
- [About](https://cuijiawei.cn/about/) - Who I am and what I work on
- [Playground](https://cuijiawei.cn/playground/) - Interactive ML demos

## Engineering

- [Advanced JavaScript - Exercises and Gotchas](https://cuijiawei.cn/2017/03/16/advanced-javascript-exercises/)
- [Connection-Oriented Transport - TCP](https://cuijiawei.cn/2017/05/18/connection-oriented-transport-tcp/)
- [Principles of Economics (Mankiw)](https://cuijiawei.cn/2017/06/26/principles-of-economics/)
- [Getting Started with iptables](https://cuijiawei.cn/2017/11/25/getting-started-with-iptables/)
- [Building a Linux Mail Server with Postfix and Dovecot](https://cuijiawei.cn/2017/12/01/linux-mail-server-postfix-dovecot/)
- [IE Quirk: Clicks on `<a>` Inside `<button>` Don't Fire](https://cuijiawei.cn/2018/04/26/ie-button-a-tag-click-fix/)

## Data

- [Kaggle & Data Science - Practical Tips and Best Practices](https://cuijiawei.cn/2025/09/15/kaggle-data-science-practical-notes/)
- [Geospatial Analysis](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/01/02/geospatial-analysis/) - Geospatial data analysis: coordinate systems, spatial joins, and the Python stack for working with maps and geometries.
- [Advanced SQL](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/01/10/advanced-sql/) - Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and indexing for analytical workloads.

## Ai

- [Deep Learning](https://cuijiawei.cn/2025/10/22/intro-to-deep-learning/) - A practical intro to deep learning: from perceptrons to backprop, CNNs, RNNs, and Transformers.
- [Game AI and Reinforcement Learning](https://cuijiawei.cn/2025/12/20/intro-to-game-ai-and-reinforcement-learning/) - How reinforcement learning powers game AI, from Q-learning and DQN to AlphaGo and AlphaZero.
- [Machine Learning Explainability](https://cuijiawei.cn/2025/12/28/machine-learning-explainability/) - Model explainability techniques: feature importance, LIME, SHAP, and the trade-off between accuracy and interpretability.
- [Computer Vision](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/01/15/computer-vision/) - Foundations of computer vision: from convolutional nets and ImageNet to transfer learning and vision transformers.

## Projects

- [wechat-dev-mcp: Bridging AI and WeChat Mini-Program Development](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/01/31/wechat-dev-mcp/)
- [Homeware Sense: Open Source Smart Home Integration for OpenClaw AI](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/02/02/introducing-homeware-sense-skill-unified-smart-home-integration-for-openclaw-ai/)
- [Deploy OpenClaw AI on Raspberry Pi: Our Open Source Guide](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/02/04/complete-raspberry-pi-openclaw-deployment-guide/)

## Llm

- [The Transformer: Attention Is All You Need](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/03/01/the-transformer-attention-is-all-you-need/)
- [BERT: Bidirectional Pretraining from Transformers](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/03/08/bert-bidirectional-pretraining/) - Pre-trained BERT with masked language modeling became the default backbone for NLP before decoder-only models took over.
- [GPT-3 and Few-Shot Learning at Scale](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/03/15/gpt-3-few-shot-learners/) - GPT-3 showed that scaling to 175B parameters enables in-context few-shot learning with no gradient updates.
- [InstructGPT: Aligning LLMs with Human Preference (RLHF)](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/03/22/instructgpt-rlhf-alignment/) - InstructGPT is the paper that turned RLHF into the standard recipe for making LLMs helpful and follow instructions — the direct ancestor of ChatGPT.
- [Chain-of-Thought Prompting: Let the Model Think Step by Step](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/03/29/chain-of-thought-prompting/) - Chain-of-thought prompting improves reasoning by eliciting intermediate steps before the final answer.
- [LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Models](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/04/05/lora-low-rank-adaptation/) - LoRA freezes the pretrained weights and learns a low-rank update, enabling cheap fine-tuning and swappable adapters.
- [FlashAttention: IO-Aware Exact Attention](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/04/12/flashattention-efficient-attention/) - FlashAttention explained clearly: IO-aware exact attention that speeds up transformers. The tiling + online-softmax trick, intuition, and code. arXiv:2205.14135
- [LLaMA: The Open-Weight Catalyst](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/04/19/llama-open-weights-revolution/) - LLaMA proved smaller models trained on more high-quality tokens can match much larger ones, igniting the open-weight era.
- [Mixtral of Experts: Sparse Mixture of Experts](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/04/26/mixtral-mixture-of-experts/) - Mixtral is a sparse mixture-of-experts model that activates only a few experts per token, combining dense-model quality with cheaper inference.
- [DeepSeek-R1: Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/05/03/deepseek-r1-reasoning-rl/) - DeepSeek-R1 explained: open-weight reasoning from pure RL, no supervised reasoning data. How it matches o1, and the recipe. arXiv:2501.12948
- [RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/05/10/rag-retrieval-augmented-generation/) - RAG explained: marry a generative model with an external knowledge index so it can look things up. The seed of every production LLM app. arXiv:2005.11401
- [ReAct: Reasoning + Acting in Language Models](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/05/17/react-reasoning-acting/) - ReAct interleaves reasoning traces and actions so a language model can plan, use tools, and observe results — the blueprint for most LLM agents today.
- [Toolformer: Language Models That Teach Themselves to Use Tools](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/05/24/toolformer-tools/) - Toolformer explained: a 6B model teaches itself to use tools from unlabeled text. The ancestor of GPT-4 function calling, with code. arXiv:2302.04761
- [Reflexion: Verbal Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/05/31/reflexion-verbal-rl/) - Reflexion explained: improve language agents with verbal RL, no weight updates, just memory of mistakes. The self-reflect loop, with code. arXiv:2303.11366
- [Generative Agents: Believable Simulacra of Human Behavior](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/06/07/generative-agents/) - Generative Agents showed that LLM-driven characters with memory, planning, and reflection produce surprisingly believable behavior — the architecture behind AI towns and sims.
- [AutoGen: Multi-Agent Conversation as Computation](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/06/14/autogen-multi-agent/) - AutoGen is a framework for building multi-agent conversations where LLM and tool-using agents collaborate to solve tasks.
- [Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/06/21/tree-of-thoughts/) - Tree of Thoughts explained: how LLMs explore, judge, and backtrack through a search tree instead of one greedy chain. Intuition + examples. arXiv:2305.10601
- [Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/06/28/scaling-laws/) - Scaling laws describe how language-model loss predictably improves as compute, data, and parameters grow — the empirical foundation for training ever-larger models.
- [RoPE: Rotary Position Embedding](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/03/rope/)
- [Chinchilla: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/05/chinchilla/) - Chinchilla established the compute-optimal scaling law: model size and training tokens should scale together, favoring far more data than was common.
- [RLHF: Deep Reinforcement Learning from Human Preferences](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/05/rlhf-from-human-preferences/) - RLHF aligns language models to human preferences by training a reward model on comparisons and optimizing the policy with PPO.
- [DPO: Direct Preference Optimization](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/07/dpo/) - DPO skips the reward model and RL loop entirely: it frames preference learning as a simple classification loss over the policy itself. Stable, cheap, and now the default alignment method.
- [Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/09/constitutional-ai/) - Constitutional AI (RLAIF) explained: how Anthropic trained harmlessness from AI feedback instead of human raters. The recipe, with code. arXiv:2212.08073
- [Self-Consistency: Improving Chain-of-Thought Reasoning](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/10/self-consistency/) - Self-Consistency explained: sample many reasoning paths, take the majority vote. The cheapest accuracy win for LLM reasoning. arXiv:2203.11171
- [CLIP: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Language Supervision](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/11/clip/) - CLIP trains image and text encoders together on 400M captioned images, enabling zero-shot classification and powering modern text-to-image and multimodal models.
- [Softmax: Turning Logits into Probabilities](https://cuijiawei.cn/2026/07/18/softmax/)
